assertions.description: Imports and instantiates MultiDirectedGraph (not MultiGraph or DirectedGraph)
assertions.evidence: import MultiDirectedGraph from 'graphology' — default import resolves to the Graph class, NOT MultiDirectedGraph. new MultiDirectedGraph() creates a plain Graph with multi:false; adding two nyc→london edges would throw at runtime.

assertions.description: Uses addEdgeWithKey (not addEdge) to create named route edges
Calls graph.edges('nyc', 'london') to list all edges between that pair
Uses getEdgeAttributes (or getEdgeAttribute) to read route attrs after listing
Uses setEdgeAttribute to update duration on 'delta-nyc-london'
Calls graph.hasEdge('delta-nyc-london') to verify the edge exists

notable_failures: uses-multidirectedgraph: Default import 'import MultiDirectedGraph from graphology' imports the Graph base class, not MultiDirectedGraph. Code silently creates a plain Graph and will throw when adding parallel edges.
